programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore two flats in Seaham in Durham, a former bank in Westhoughton near Manchester and a building with a shop and residential space in Surbiton in Surrey. All three are sold under the hammer and their new owners explain their ambitious plans to add value to their acquisitions(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In Bridge of Allan, Karen and her 75-year-old father go into business together combining the ancient craft of glassblowing and Scotland's famous tipple, creating an unique range of whisky gifts. With the aid of a £25,000 loan the business is continuing to keep a dying art alive. Plus, a transgender entrepreneur takes control of her future after struggling with employment(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to northern Lincolnshire to meet the volunteers from Immingham in Bloom. They've been taking part in Britain In Bloom for seven years and have won gold before. However, this time the group is upping the ante by taking a huge risk. Chris finds out how they plan to retain their crown by including the port itself in their entry, with plans to brighten this enormous grey industrial area with colourful flowers(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blues go bargain hunting in Lincolnshire, with the help of experts Nick Hall and Gary Pe. Plus, presenter Charlie Ross learns about learns about the rules on finding treasure at the bottom of the garden(n)

While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the 1937 case of 23-year-old Ruby Keen, who was found strangled to death with her own scarf in an alleyway in Leighton Buzzard, Bedfordshire. Ex-boyfriend Leslie Stone was found guilty of murder and hanged. More than 80 years later, Stone's nieces cannot believe their uncle committed such a heinous crime and want to learn more about his character and the case, which was kept a secret from them by their mother(n)

programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Colourful milkshake funnel cakes have all the fun of fresh doughnuts with none of the fuss. A giant time-saving cookie is so easy it's fried in the pan, and a glistening nutty baklava looks so impressive but is completely fool-proof. programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Warrington Wolves (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents live coverage of the showpiece fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium, as Saints take on the Wire for the famous trophy. St Helens will be confident their first Challenge Cup final appearance since their 2008 victory will be a successful one given their domination of Super League this season. Hooker James Roby is the only survivor from that Saints success and he was in vintage form during their 26-2 semi-final win over Halifax. But Warrington are unlikely to go down without a fight, and have been St Helens' closest rivals through much of the domestic league campaign. However, since their Challenge Cup victory in 2012 they have lost their last six consecutive finals in league and cup. Can the likes of key man Blake Austin see them end that losing run at Wembley this weekend?(n)

programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Arsenal at Anfield and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. Jurgen Klopp's side had an excellent recent record against the Gunners, having gone undefeated against them since April 2015. Last season's corresponding fixture saw the Reds run out 5-1 winners, with a Roberto Firmino hat-trick doing most of the damage. programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.desc: Adam Frost dispels the confusion of the summer pruning of wisteria, and demonstrates an ingenious solution to planting in dry shade under trees. Arit Anderson is in Germany finding out about the incredible transformation of a disused industrial site into a thriving and beautiful park, beloved by visitors and the local community. Rachel de Thame heads to Gloucestershire to find out about the 100-year history of the gardens at Kiftsgate Court, and Nick Bailey profiles the many varieties of summer flowering clematis(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Niall Strawson and Ferne Corrigan(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: A Nottingham couple and two friends from Warrington, Cheshire, sample new ways of life as they exchange homes. They then decide whether they have enjoyed the experience. Presented by Gloria Hunniford(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: While on the verge of finding heirs in line to a significant inheritance from John Clench, Michael Buerk and the Heir Hunters uncover a family secret that throws everything on its head, diverting the money in a different direction - but a family reunion makes it all worthwhile. The case of Roy Rogerson reveals a man with a passion for cricket and uncovers the fascinating but dangerous career path of his grandfather, leaving the heirs desperate to find out more(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Sally Mack lacks the confidence to cook for anyone except herself, but after turning 50 she decided that had to change. Lorraine creates a Chinese-inspired menu of dishes that are simple to prepare, including sweet-and-sour ribs, chicken and cashew nut stir fry and a showstopping cake(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the British tradition of cooking with food hunted from the land and prepare a selection of dishes using rabbit, grouse and venison(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Naturalist Chris Packham observes the devious tactics and sneaky tricks that some animals carry out in order to survive. New, ground-breaking science helps reveal astonishing visual trickery, from illusionist zebras to vanishing geckos and shape-shifting cuttlefish. Mischievous mimics, from snakes to baby birds disguised as caterpillars, and a lioness who looks like a male lion are also highlighted. The world's ultimate con artists can even rely on sneaky strategies against their own kind, such as the monkey that cheats its family and the lizard that fakes its death(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: New expert Angus Ashworth takes the pilgrims' watery route over the sea to the Holy Island of Lindisfarne and veteran Tripper David Harper hoofs it up the longest avenue in Scotland at Marchmont House. The duo then head towards the finishing line at an auction in Alnwick, Northumberland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Chris Rogers, Helen Fospero, Ricky Andalcio, Miranda Krestovnikoff and Kevin Duala form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Northern Ireland, where they find out about the invention of the ejector seat, and visit Armagh Observatory, a railway museum located inside a barber shop, and SS Nomadic, known as Titanic's little sister(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on specialised subjects including the Thirty Years' War, the members and music of Genesis, Shakespeare's Elizabethan tragedies, and Joss Whedon's film Serenity and Firefly TV series. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the bank holiday weekend looming, Monty Don has plenty of inspiration for gardeners from planting for late summer colour to tidying up herbs and pruning soft fruit. Nick Bailey concludes his journey into the clematis world by looking at the late summer-flowering varieties and the programme travels to Devon to meet a retired biology teacher with a passion for houseleeks(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: As the high summer of 1973 ebbs away, Bob and girlfriend Ann Reinking hire a weekend house in the country to help comfort the recently bereaved Neil Simon. Also invited are Gwen and her boyfriend Ron. Ann is concerned that Gwen won't like her, but Bob says Gwen will be on her best behaviour for Neil. Gwen proves to be in buoyant mood, excited to announce a theatre has been secured for their project of Chicago, but Bob is hesitant claiming he has a lot of work on with the Lenny script. Thus, new tensions emerge. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star(n)

credits.actor: Sam Rockwell, Michelle Williams

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee, Rhod Gilbert and Ed Byrne join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig looks at pathology, the causal study of disease based on the analysis of bodily fluids. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: BBC Ouch
programme.desc: Chris McCausland introduces this comedy and storytelling show, featuring funny and fascinating stories told by disabled people and those with mental health difficulties. This year's theme, Lost and Found, is particularly revealing(n)

programme.title: The Conjoined Twins: An Impossible Decision
programme.desc: Conjoined twins Marieme and Ndeye have separate hearts and lungs, but Marieme's heart is very weak and her life expectancy is poor and if she dies, her stronger sister will die with her. As the girls' health deteriorates, their father Ibrahima and staff at Great Ormond Street Hospital are forced to decide whether a separation should be considered. This programme tracks the journey of Ibrahima and the medical team as they wrestle with is a seemingly impossible decision with the help of the hospital's Clinical Ethics Committee(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Celebrating Notting Hill Carnival with Caribbean music, amazing costumes and tasty food(n)

programme.title: Deadly Top 10
programme.desc: Steve Backshall chooses his favourite flying predators, including a wasp and a vulture(n)

programme.title: The Animal's Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines Britain from an animal's point of view as he tries to understand the needs, problems and histories of native species. He begins with freshwater creatures, visiting the Highlands, where he witnesses a fishing display by ospreys and learns how to tempt them to nest further south. He also investigates what white-faced darter dragonflies, black water voles and brown trout can reveal about the changing nature of waterways(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Six more robots compete in the second heat, with last year's champion Carbide returning to face brand new challengers Eruption, Aftershock, Crackers N Smash, Gabriel 2 and Big Nipper. Dara Ó Briain and Angela Scanlon present the show, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond's bull Major returns to work after being injured earlier in the year, but getting him to the field of cows involves a soggy detour. Raymond also travels to Switzerland with fiancee Jen to help their friends gather their valais blacknose sheep from the mountaintops. Kirsty and Aimee go sheep shopping to double their flock, while bad news forces buffalo farmer Stevie to make a shocking decision(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Makers of cooking sauces compete against each other for a place in the final, as they test out their products on locals in Malhamdale, in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. Joe, a teacher from Runcorn, spices things up with his home made hot sauce, while Spanish-born Beatriz makes her 'paella in a bottle'. Alice sells a salted caramel sauce made in her kitchen in Northern Ireland, and Kiran from Solihull offers up her healthy take on curry sauce(n)

programme.title: Secret Britain
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Adam Henson explore the secrets of the Scottish Highlands. Adam climbs the north face of Ben Nevis, a spectacular rock ascent on which few tourists venture, and lives out his boyhood dream on a Wild West-style cattle ranch. Ellie probes a monster mystery in Britain's deepest water Loch Morar, visits an ancient shrine to healing on the Black Isle, and teams up with Adam to go hunting for Bonnie Prince Charlie's cave hideaway, before they take a magical ride on the real Hogwarts Express(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin follow the progress of auction properties in Folkestone in Kent, Hanley, Stoke-on-Trent and Seascale in Cumbria(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at the Royal Mansour in Marrakesh, Morocco, which was built by royal decree to showcase the region to world leaders, as well as billionaires and celebrity guests. Monica works with executive housekeeper Laurence, who shows her each of the 350 pairs of curtains must be caressed by hand to create perfect pleats, while Giles dons the uniform of the hotel butlers as he is introduced him to the hotel's underground tunnels, which ensure absolute discretion to the guests(n)

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin and the team journey south to the oldest islands in the Galapagos to see the impact humans have had on this pristine wilderness. Moving on to the island of Isabela, Liz descends into a vertical lava cave, then makes her final land-based stop on Santa Cruz, where she meets a man on a mission to protect the giant tortoise population. Finally, she dives into the deep blue waters to witness the birth of a new island(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland. With analysis by Martin Keown and Karen Carney(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On one of the hottest days of the year, the Dover RNLI crew race to a stricken Channel swimmer enduring difficulties within eyesight of the French coast. In Mudeford, there are growing fears for a dinghy sailor four miles out in rough seas and refusing to accept help. And the Tenby team are called to a runaway yacht which is on the verge of running aground(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef's latest culinary travels take him through Mexico City, and Puebla, where he samples Mexico's most iconic dish, the meat stew mole. Later, in the deserts of Oaxaca, he discovers how one of the country's most famous drinks, mezcal is made(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by tenor Allan Clayton, soprano Nadine Benjamin and beatboxer Jason Singh as the programme focuses on 'the voice'. Tenor Jeffrey Lloyd-Roberts investigates different singing styles and Tom Service takes maestro Semyon Bychkov on a musical car journey in an Austin Maestro. There's also a genre-mixing finale as Jason samples Nadine's voice for a bandstand performance(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Ireland. John Inverdale presents action from the World Cup warm-up fixture, which took place at Twickenham, as Ireland looked to avenge the 32-20 defeat they suffered against England on the opening weekend of the 2019 Six Nations in Dublin. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon are challenged to a cricket match by Warden Hodges, who has signed up a professional for his side. Featuring a guest appearance by former England and Yorkshire fast bowler Fred Trueman. Vintage comedy first shown in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and Bill Pertwee(n)

credits.actor: Arthur Lowe, Bill Pertwee

programme.title: Viceroy's House
programme.desc: The final Viceroy of India, Lord Mountbatten, oversees the transition of British India to independence, but meets with conflict as opposing sides clash in the face of huge change. Drama, starring Hugh Bonneville, Gillian Anderson and Manish Dayal(n)

credits.actor: Hugh Bonneville, Gillian Anderson, Manish Dayal

programme.title: 20th Century Women
programme.desc: Comedy drama following a teenage boy, his mother and two other women who help raise him among the love and freedom of Southern California in 1979. Starring Annette Bening(n)

credits.actor: Annette Bening

programme.title: Hunky Dory
programme.desc: Idealistic drama teacher Vivienne is an outsider among the cynical staff and apathetic pupils of a 1970s south Wales school. Undeterred by general disinterest and sweltering heat, she sets out to inspire her class to stage a rock musical based on Shakespeare's The Tempest. Comedy drama, with Minnie Driver and Aneurin Barnard(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter T, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the bank holiday weekend looming, Monty Don has plenty of inspiration for gardeners from planting for late summer colour to tidying up herbs and pruning soft fruit. Nick Bailey concludes his journey into the clematis world by looking at the late summer-flowering varieties and the programme travels to Devon to meet a retired biology teacher with a passion for houseleeks(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits the Lake District to take a look at some of the jobs working animals do. He joins the Cockermouth Mountain Rescue team to see search-and-rescue dogs in action and meets 15-week-old Jura, a border collie pup learning the ropes from the older dogs. He then meets George Newton and his two Dales ponies, Charlie and George. They work the forests for timber as they're able to get to places that normal machinery can't. But Sean is surprised to find that the biggest job is being done by the smallest creatures - beetles(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson shows how to create an evergreen hanging basket, George Anderson takes a look at the bedding flower display and Carole Baxter starts sowing over the winter vegetables(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater: Eating Together
programme.desc: Nigel visits three home cooks to learn about well-loved soup recipes that have come from around the world. Miles tells him how his mother brought her recipe for spicy fish tea with her when she moved to Wiltshire from Jamaica in the 1960s, while Sumiko gives him a lesson in the art of Japanese miso soup. Finally, Nigel meets 87-year-old Regina at her home in Leeds to find out how she makes a classic Jewish chicken soup, joining her family for a Friday night Shabbat dinner(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Four more producers with fledgling businesses test out their products on the locals in Malhamdale, in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. This time it's the turn of meat producers fighting it out for a place in the final. They include oil-rig worker Mark, who prepares and cures bacon from home in Aberdeenshire(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Ireland. John Inverdale presents action from the World Cup warm-up fixture, which took place at Twickenham, as Ireland looked to avenge the 32-20 defeat they suffered against England on the opening weekend of the 2019 Six Nations in Dublin. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Meeting de Paris. Action from the 12th meeting of the season, held at Stade Sebastien Charlety, where sprint star Noah Lyles was among the elite athletes expected to be on show(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage from Alexandra Stadium in Birmingham, where athletes compete for national honours and places in the squad for this year's World Championships in Doha. Dina Asher-Smith is expected to be a medal hope in Qatar in both the 100m and 200m, and competes in the latter today, while another global contender in Laura Muir is set to run in the 1500m(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel more than 120 miles north of the Arctic Circle into Swedish Lapland to work in Icehotel in the village of Jukkasjarvi. Using two-ton blocks of ice hacked from the nearby River Torne, Giles works with a team of international artists to create individually crafted rooms and is taught how to make drinking glasses from ice, while Monica learns how the head chef uses natural ingredients found in the nearby forest; and meets Manne, an 82-year-old man who supplies reindeer meat to the hotel(n)

programme.title: The Ganges with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: The comedian and presenter embarks on an epic journey along the sacred river, from its source in the Himalayas to the Bay of Bengal. She begins by trekking to the source of the Ganges River in the highest mountain range on Earth, meeting hermits and wandering holy people that call this sacred landscape home. Moving on, she visits Rishikesh - a magnet for spiritual-seekers ever since the Beatles stayed there almost 50 years ago - meets a holy man-turned-business guru and attends a nightly ceremony to praise the river(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a mattress factory in Leeds, where he discovers how steel is stretched and coiled into springs and the advantages of natural fibres like hemp and wool. Cherry Healey talks to a sleep scientist about the benefits to reaction time from taking an afternoon nap, and Ruth Goodman tries out a Middle Ages mattress made out of a straw-stuffed sack(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of an eco-friendly entrepreneur's alternative to straws, a range of skincare products, a device designed to make bikes more visible, and a brand of food with a personal story behind it. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mobeen Azhar investigates the murder of Larnell Bruce by a member of a white supremacist gang in Portland, and assesses the degree to which race played a role in the choice of victim. The investigation delves into the history of the seemingly liberal city, and uncovers a dark underbelly of racial tensions in the state of Oregon. The programme also examines the events of the trial, which were marked by clashes between anti-fascist groups and white supremacists on the streets. Featuring interviews with members of the militant groups and expert witnesses called on to testify(n)

programme.title: Peter Taylor: My Journey Through the Troubles
programme.desc: Journalist Peter Taylor presents a documentary marking the 50th anniversary of the deployment of British troops in August 1969. Peter reflects on his experiences of covering the Northern Ireland conflict for almost a half century. Peter has written a number of books on political violence, exploring the Troubles from British, Loyalist and Republican points of view. This film is a highly personal account of the key events of the Troubles and the enduring legacies of the conflict, drawing on Peter's experiences of reporting from the region(n)

programme.title: Pioneer
programme.desc: An American oil company plans to build an underwater pipeline off the coast of Norway, and hires a deep-sea diver to work on the project. However, when the operation is struck by a tragic accident, he becomes suspicious of his employers' true objectives. Thriller, starring Aksel Hennie, Wes Bentley and Stephen Lang. In Norwegian and English(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.sub-title: BBC Proms: Rattle Conducts Belshazzar's Feast
programme.desc: At the Royal Albert Hall, Walton's choral masterpiece Belshazzar's Feast gets the Proms treatment with conductor Simon Rattle, the London Symphony Orchestra, the LSO Chorus, the Orfeo Catala Choir and Orfeo Catala Youth Choir and Canadian baritone Gerald Finley as soloist. Plus, Varese's celebration of the modern city in sound Amériques and Charles Koechlin's Jungle Book-inspired Les bandar-log. Presented by Suzy Klein(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure, lead singer of Ultravox and one of the men behind Band Aid, and Kim Appleby, who had a string of hits with her sister Mel in the Stock, Aitken & Waterman-produced band Mel and Kim, revisit the 1980s to determine why certain cities produced their own diverse tunes. In the first episode, they explore London's New Romantic movement, travel to Coventry to investigate the rise of Ska and speak to some of Sheffield's electronic music pioneers(n)

programme.title: Snow Patrol - Ward Park 3
programme.desc: Snow Patrol perform a homecoming gig at Ward Park in Bangor, Co Down, as the band go on a journey through their early classics to songs from their most recent album. Celebrating 25 years in music, the rockers will be treating the crowd to hits from their breakthrough release Final Straw and its follow-up Eyes Open, and last year's album Wildness(n)

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Music promoter John Giddings tells the story of how live performance has become a billion-pound industry. Where once bands would tour just to promote an album, in the age of downloads and dwindling record sales, the live arena is booming. The programme also tells the story of the original Isle of Wight festival, where a bunch of young promoters with big ideas persuaded Bob Dylan, the Who and Leonard Cohen to perform. The event transformed the music industry, and, for the young John who was in the audience, inspired a whole career. Contributors include Phil Collins, Stewart Copeland, Mel C and Alex James(n)

programme.title: The Irish Rock Story: A Tale of Two Cities
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how rock music evolved in Ireland over 40 years. The film demonstrates how the sounds that emerged were grounded in the musical traditions of the island's two main cities, from the pioneering touring showbands of the late 1950s through to the modern day. Richard Dormer narrates an examination of how the harder sounds of Belfast merged with the more folk-centric traditions of Dublin, celebrates some of the key musicians to have emerged from the Irish scene, and reveals how they were influenced by the political, social and cultural climate of their surroundings. With contributions from U2, Sinead O'Connor and Bob Geldof(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas Shelby's long-awaited day has arrived, but in the middle of the celebrations, a mysterious visitor puts the entire Shelby family in danger, pulling the mob boss into a web of intrigue more lethal than anything he has yet encountered. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

credits.actor: Cillian Murphy

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: On the orders of the secretive Economic League, Tommy goes to London to meet the wildly eccentric and volatile Duke Leon - and when he is given his mission, he soon realises the extreme lengths his new paymasters are willing to go to in their quest for power. Back in Birmingham, John's temper gets the better of him, and a personal dispute with the Italians threatens to explode into a devastating war. Tommy struggles to regain control of the situation - only to discover how extensive his new enemy's hold on the Peaky Blinders is. Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Joe Cole star, with an appearance by Paddy Considine(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy responds to the Italians' actions, setting him on a path of deadly vengeance that threatens to take him to his darkest place yet - and could also splinter the family. And when he meets Grand Duchess Izabella, the real force behind his mission, he discovers there's a traitor in his paymasters' midst - one who could unravel the entire plan. Meanwhile, Polly realises the depths of her new friend's intentions for her, and Linda implores an increasingly guilty Arthur to give up the criminal life(n)

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: With the team's explorations of New Guinea drawing to a close, George McGavin visits an erupting volcano, where he encounters a rare bird that depends on the hot ash to survive. Back at the original site, Steve Backshall and Gordon Buchanan head deep into the crater and discover two new mammals - a giant rat and a tree-climbing marsupial(n)

programme.title: The Private Life of Cows
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty reveals the hidden lives of farmyard animals, beginning with an investigation into the nature of cows. Visiting Devon and Northumberland, he discovers how the hierarchy of a herd is established, tests their intelligence by trying to teach three animals how to demand food using a bell, and discovers what could turn a placid creature into a potential killer(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: SP and Simon finally have a lead on the whereabouts of June's phone, while Philip is told that the hostages have been booby-trapped - with Yusuf threatening to detonate. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: As the ferry docks in Provestenen, Beate greets Philip - who sees June chasing Rami in the distance. Grabbing a gun he follows them into a dockside warehouse. In Danish(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.sub-title: This Is What We Do (July 1967 - December 1967)
programme.desc: Hanoi lays plans for a massive surprise offensive, while the Johnson administration attempts to reassure the American public that victory is in sight(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: On the eve of the Tet holiday, North Vietnamese and Viet Cong forces launch surprise attacks throughout the south, while Lyndon Johnson decides not to stand for re-election(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Gary Davies introduce the edition first aired on May 19, featuring Prefab Sprout, Kylie Minogue, Derek B, Aztec Camera, Heart, Ofra Haza, Hothouse Flowers, Debbie Gibson, Belinda Carlisle, Billy Bragg with Cara Tivey, Wet Wet Wet and Liverpool FC(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The gripping drama is halfway through and the twists and turns come thick and fast as Tommy shares some important family news with his brothers, and outlines his plans for the most audacious criminal act the gang has ever undertaken. He also finds himself playing a dangerous game with Tatiana as they attempt to acquire information from each another. Meanwhile, a guilty Polly makes an ill-advised confession - with terrible consequences for her nephew. Cillian Murphy stars, with Helen McCrory, Paddy Considine and Gaite Jansen(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Russians test the Shelbys and their gang, and Tommy begins to realise he may have underestimated his new business partners, and has now been seriously outmanoeuvred. As aristocrats exiled by the Soviet Revolution, the Petrovnas have absolutely nothing to lose, and beneath their civilised veneer lies a fierce determination to regain what they have lost. However, the Birmingham mobster still has an ace up his sleeve, in the form of an old enemy now working as an ally. The question is, can he control him? Tom Hardy returns as menacing mobster Alfie Solomons, with Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory, Annabelle Wallis and Paul Anderson(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The third run of Steven Knight's period drama reaches its final episode, and yet again Cillian Murphy as Thomas Shelby finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation. Has his criminal life finally caught up with the Birmingham-based mobster? Tommy prepares to commit the most audacious crime of his career, only to receive an unexpected blow that puts him under unbearable pressure. In a nerve-shredding race against time, Tommy is forced to confront hard truths about himself and his family that could change the Peaky Blinders for ever. As he faces his worst fears he needs help more than ever - but is increasingly uncertain about who he can trust(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Sheku Kanneh-Mason performs Elgar's Cello Concerto at the Royal Albert Hall, with the City of Birmingham Symphony Orchestra and their Music Director Mirga Gražinyte-Tyla. Introduced by Tom Service and Sheku's sister, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason(n)

programme.title: Peter Rice: An Engineer Imagines
programme.desc: A tribute to the life and ideas of Peter Rice, one of the most distinguished structural engineers of the late 20th century. The film traces his career from his native Ireland through London, Sydney and Paris, revealing how he pushed the boundaries of art and science, until to his untimely death in 1992(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson

When the truth comes out about why his father really left, Jamie lashes out, threatening to ruin everything all over again. Mildred receives a singing teddy from home and it makes her feel homesick. She doesn't even manage to turn Ethel back successfully - she still has a pig's snout! programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sasha is forced to work with her ex-husband Ned Hancock when their teams investigate the death of film critic Oliver Houghton, whose body was discovered floating in the Thames. UCOS delves into the victim's past and explores his link to the murder of a conceptual artist 20 years previously, while Hancock's knowledge of London's hidden past proves invaluable when a piece of occult footage from the 1970s plunges the investigators into the macabre world of human sacrifice. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance by Barnaby Kay(n)

programme.title: The Truth About Meat
programme.desc: Greengrocer Chris Bavin sets out to find out whether certain claims about meat, linking it to cancer and heart disease, are justified. With the help of a team of scientists, he follows 40 volunteers on a study to find out whether there are any potential dangers, tests whether paying more for chicken makes it any better, and discovers a way to dramatically reduce the health risks associated with processed meats. Plus, an unlikely lean super-meat that will not break the bank(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Tina Daheley present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Andi Oliver is joined by chefs Theo Randall and Freddy Forster, and special guest Matt Lucas. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Colourful milkshake funnel cakes have all the fun of fresh doughnuts with none of the fuss. A giant time-saving cookie is so easy it's fried in the pan, and a glistening nutty baklava looks so impressive but is completely fool-proof. On the road, Nadiya joins the team Tate and Lyle on the River Thames, to find out how they produce one of her favourite fast-tracks to flavour - golden syrup(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Martin Keown look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Liverpool v Arsenal and Manchester United v Crystal Palace in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw oversees proceedings in Lincoln as a father-and-daughter team takes on a mother-and-daughter duo, each buying three items in the hope of making the most profit at auction. Experts Kate Bliss and Raj Bisram are on hand to advise(n)

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Warrington Wolves (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents live coverage of the showpiece fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium, as Saints take on the Wire for the famous trophy. St Helens will be confident their first Challenge Cup final appearance since their 2008 victory will be a successful one given their domination of Super League this season. Hooker James Roby is the only survivor from that Saints success and he was in vintage form during their 26-2 semi-final win over Halifax. But Warrington are unlikely to go down without a fight, and have been St Helens' closest rivals through much of the domestic league campaign. However, since their Challenge Cup victory in 2012 they have lost their last six consecutive finals in league and cup. Can the likes of key man Blake Austin see them end that losing run at Wembley this weekend?(n)

He must use his new skills to carry out a special mission - helping the inventor to steal back his miniaturisation technology from a ruthless businessman who intends to turn it into a deadly weapon. Superhero adventure, starring Paul Rudd, Michael Douglas and Evangeline Lilly(n)

programme.title: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: Game show in which two pairs of complete strangers team up, each battling to outdo the other by listing as much as they know about a given subject. The first couple to win two rounds get to play for £50,000 and each time the champions defeat a new set of opponents their winnings mount up(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ethan struggles to cope in the aftermath of the incident at the market, and decides to throw himself into his work. When Theo, an old friend from university, arrives at the emergency department with his daughter Effie, who has Cystic Fibrosis, Ethan puts himself on the line to give her a chance. After the loss of his mother, Rash decided to delay his return to work, but soon finds himself helping a mother and baby in desperate need of care. Meanwhile, Duffy worries about the future(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Arsenal at Anfield and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. Jurgen Klopp's side had an excellent recent record against the Gunners, having gone undefeated against them since April 2015. Last season's corresponding fixture saw the Reds run out 5-1 winners, with a Roberto Firmino hat-trick doing most of the damage. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Southampton at the Amex Stadium, Norwich City v Chelsea at Carrow Road, Sheffield United v Leicester City at Bramall Lane, Watford v West Ham United at Vicarage Road and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley at Molineux. With analysis by Ian Wright and Danny Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: Seven unsigned MCs compete to become the next rap superstar, as they go head-to-head in a battle to sign for rap legends Krept and Konan's record label. Each week the artists are set two tests to find out who has the strongest song writing props and stage presence. In the first week, the contestants are challenged to rap over the beat that made Krept and Konan household names(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum explores the Yorkshire Dales on a motortrike and meets the Christian woman behind the tours, and also visits Wensleydale Creamery to learn about the cheese(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and Phil are determined to fulfill their dream of retiring to the sun together, but find themselves continually hampered by logistical difficulties. Edith is finding her irritating sister Joan very difficult to accommodate, and Roger, impatient to see Phil sell his house, decides to help the process along by checking his roof for dry rot. Wendy drops in on Edith and mentions that she is planning a children's book, and despite not yet knowing what the plotline will be, thinks it may involve a complicated family. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and Phil discover a bookshop hosting local author Wendy Stevens, conducting a signing of her inaugural book, The Lemming Family. However, Phil is later outraged to find out that the characters are thinly disguised portraits of himself and the family. Roger's hopes of becoming a Social Services-supported carer look like being revived, though not quite in the way he imagines when the infamous Mrs Pool makes a reappearance. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple find a retirement home in the Derbyshire Peak District, and also meets a master armourer and his apprentice upholding the tradition of the craft(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. In an effort to confront their money-spending ways, Alex and Steph resort to shock tactics, and set up a pop-up-shop in the family home displaying all of their biggest spends, with shocking results. Can Alex and Steph help this family start saving, rather than spending?(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn head to Ferryside, a close-knit coastal community just eight miles from Carmarthen in south-west Wales. They want to celebrate the work and achievements of Ferryside Lifeboat, a completely independent organisation run entirely by volunteers, and prepare a spectacular fishy feast, cooked in a giant fire pit on the beach(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: Return of the programme in which Kate and her Welsh sheepdog Teg explore Wales' countryside. She begins in North Wales, trying to climb the tallest man-made structure in the country to fix some loose cables on the Arfon Transmission Tower. Next, Kate learns how to overcome natural obstacles with the novel activity of packrafting - using a lightweight inflatable canoe. On the north-east coast, Kate meets a pack of dogs trained to carry out wildlife conservation by sniffing out endangered species, then on the north-west coast she digs and prepares a grave for a new resident who in death wants to give himself to help nature thrive(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a 1980s edition of the celebrity quiz, featuring four teams of famous faces from the decade seeking unlikely answers. The contestants are athletes Fatima Whitbread and Robin Cousins, Doctor Who stars Colin Baker and Nicola Bryant, Blue Peter presenters Mark Curry and Peter Duncan, and singers Sonia and Jaki Graham(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker heads to Warwickshire to marks the centenary of the death of Joseph Arch, a farm labourer who helped form the National Agricultural Labourers' Union in 1872 and later became an MP. Elsewhere, Adam Henson battles against the elements to get his harvest safely home, and John Craven, Cerys Matthews and Simon King reveal the final 12 pictures the viewers will get to vote on in this year's Countryfile photographic competition.(n)

programme.title: Poldark
programme.desc: Demelza discovers the French are smuggling arms into Cornwall, but Ross is determined to take revenge on Merceron and Hanson for framing Ned and returns to London to confront them. Sam continues to meet with Tess against all caution as Cecily and Geoffrey Charles plan their escape with Ross's help, but the danger that stalks the Poldarks follows swiftly(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Wall Street Crash brings the Shelby family back together when the implications for the family business become clear. Meanwhile, Tommy's political career is going from strength to strength, but its is not long before ghosts from his past threaten to undermine his position. Return of the period drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Helen McCrory(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, which were Bournemouth v Manchester City at Vitality Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Newcastle United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Bournemouth were looking for a first-ever win over the Citizens, while Spurs were had won their last four matches against the Magpies. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Peter Crouch(n)

programme.title: Mavericks: Sport's Lost Heroes
programme.sub-title: Shades: The Life and Times of Chris Hallam
programme.desc: Documentary paying tribute to sporting heroes from the past, beginning with Chris Hallam, the wheelchair racer from south Wales who shone a light on Paralympic sport(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore two flats in Seaham in Durham, a former bank in Westhoughton near Manchester and a building with a shop and residential space in Surbiton in Surrey. All three are sold under the hammer and their new owners explain their ambitious plans to add value to their acquisitions(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In Bridge of Allan, Karen and her 75-year-old father go into business together combining the ancient craft of glassblowing and Scotland's famous tipple, creating an unique range of whisky gifts. With the aid of a £25,000 loan the business is continuing to keep a dying art alive. Plus, a transgender entrepreneur takes control of her future after struggling with employment(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to northern Lincolnshire to meet the volunteers from Immingham in Bloom. They've been taking part in Britain In Bloom for seven years and have won gold before. However, this time the group is upping the ante by taking a huge risk. Chris finds out how they plan to retain their crown by including the port itself in their entry, with plans to brighten this enormous grey industrial area with colourful flowers(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blues go bargain hunting in Lincolnshire, with the help of experts Nick Hall and Gary Pe. Plus, presenter Charlie Ross learns about learns about the rules on finding treasure at the bottom of the garden(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win a £10,000 jackpot, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Welsh Borders with a retired couple of keen gardeners who have £500,000 to plough into buying a peaceful home in the country. While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the 1937 case of 23-year-old Ruby Keen, who was found strangled to death with her own scarf in an alleyway in Leighton Buzzard, Bedfordshire. Ex-boyfriend Leslie Stone was found guilty of murder and hanged. More than 80 years later, Stone's nieces cannot believe their uncle committed such a heinous crime and want to learn more about his character and the case, which was kept a secret from them by their mother(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: World Pipe Band Championships
programme.desc: Jackie Bird presents coverage of the event from Glasgow Green. This edition features some of the best performances from the Grade One arena, with commentary and analysis from respected piper Bob Worrall, as 15 bands from around the world compete for the coveted title(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel demands an explanation from the police about how Hunter escaped - and insists they find her son safely. Rainie and Max argue about their arrangements over Abi. The foul-mouthed matriarch also clashes with Father Damien when he raises religious concerns about the ceremony - and to top it all off, she gets more than she bargained for when she invites Cathy's boyfriend's parents over for the evening. Edith is finding her irritating sister Joan very difficult to accommodate, and Roger, impatient to see Phil sell his house, decides to help the process along by checking his roof for dry rot. Wendy drops in on Edith and mentions that she is planning a children's book, and despite not yet knowing what the plotline will be, thinks it may involve a complicated family. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and Phil discover a bookshop hosting local author Wendy Stevens, conducting a signing of her inaugural book, The Lemming Family. However, Phil is later outraged to find out that the characters are thinly disguised portraits of himself and the family. Roger's hopes of becoming a Social Services-supported carer look like being revived, though not quite in the way he imagines when the infamous Mrs Pool makes a reappearance. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. In an effort to confront their money-spending ways, Alex and Steph resort to shock tactics, and set up a pop-up-shop in the family home displaying all of their biggest spends, with shocking results. Can Alex and Steph help this family start saving, rather than spending?(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a 1980s edition of the celebrity quiz, featuring four teams of famous faces from the decade seeking unlikely answers. The contestants are athletes Fatima Whitbread and Robin Cousins, Doctor Who stars Colin Baker and Nicola Bryant, Blue Peter presenters Mark Curry and Peter Duncan, and singers Sonia and Jaki Graham(n)

programme.title: The Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates highlights of the event held on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle, while Aasmah Mir goes behind-the-scenes to meet some of the performers and get a flavour of how this enormous event comes together. The British Army is at the fore, with the bands of the Scots Guards, the Irish Guards and the Royal Regiment of Scotland, complemented by international performers including the New Zealand Army Band, the Tobago Defence Force Steel Orchestra and the Beijing Marching Wind Band and Cultural Display Team(n)

All three are sold under the hammer and their new owners explain their ambitious plans to add value to their acquisitions(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In Bridge of Allan, Karen and her 75-year-old father go into business together combining the ancient craft of glassblowing and Scotland's famous tipple, creating an unique range of whisky gifts. With the aid of a £25,000 loan the business is continuing to keep a dying art alive. Plus, a transgender entrepreneur takes control of her future after struggling with employment(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to northern Lincolnshire to meet the volunteers from Immingham in Bloom. They've been taking part in Britain In Bloom for seven years and have won gold before. However, this time the group is upping the ante by taking a huge risk. Chris finds out how they plan to retain their crown by including the port itself in their entry, with plans to brighten this enormous grey industrial area with colourful flowers(n)

While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the 1937 case of 23-year-old Ruby Keen, who was found strangled to death with her own scarf in an alleyway in Leighton Buzzard, Bedfordshire. Ex-boyfriend Leslie Stone was found guilty of murder and hanged. More than 80 years later, Stone's nieces cannot believe their uncle committed such a heinous crime and want to learn more about his character and the case, which was kept a secret from them by their mother(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sasha is forced to work with her ex-husband Ned Hancock when their teams investigate the death of film critic Oliver Houghton, whose body was discovered floating in the Thames. UCOS delves into the victim's past and explores his link to the murder of a conceptual artist 20 years previously, while Hancock's knowledge of London's hidden past proves invaluable when a piece of occult footage from the 1970s plunges the investigators into the macabre world of human sacrifice. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance by Barnaby Kay(n)

programme.title: My Old Lady
programme.desc: A penniless New Yorker hopes to turn his fortunes around by selling his dead father's flat in Paris, only to find an eccentric elderly Englishwoman is living there - and under French law, he cannot sell until after she dies. As he gets to know his unexpected tenant, he discovers a side to his father that he never know. Comedy drama based on director Israel Horovitz's stage play, starring Maggie Smith, Kevin Kline and Kristin Scott Thomas(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Colourful milkshake funnel cakes have all the fun of fresh doughnuts with none of the fuss. A giant time-saving cookie is so easy it's fried in the pan, and a glistening nutty baklava looks so impressive but is completely fool-proof. programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Warrington Wolves (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents live coverage of the showpiece fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium, as Saints take on the Wire for the famous trophy. St Helens will be confident their first Challenge Cup final appearance since their 2008 victory will be a successful one given their domination of Super League this season. Hooker James Roby is the only survivor from that Saints success and he was in vintage form during their 26-2 semi-final win over Halifax. But Warrington are unlikely to go down without a fight, and have been St Helens' closest rivals through much of the domestic league campaign. However, since their Challenge Cup victory in 2012 they have lost their last six consecutive finals in league and cup. Can the likes of key man Blake Austin see them end that losing run at Wembley this weekend?(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Arsenal at Anfield and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. Jurgen Klopp's side had an excellent recent record against the Gunners, having gone undefeated against them since April 2015. Last season's corresponding fixture saw the Reds run out 5-1 winners, with a Roberto Firmino hat-trick doing most of the damage. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Southampton at the Amex Stadium, Norwich City v Chelsea at Carrow Road, Sheffield United v Leicester City at Bramall Lane, Watford v West Ham United at Vicarage Road and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley at Molineux. programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues that judges - especially those of the European Court of Human Rights - have usurped power by expanding the interpretation of human rights law. programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Members of the public compete alongside celebrities including Jake Quickenden, Dom Lever, Katie Walsh and Lee Latchford-Evans. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK(n)

programme.title: Harry Hill's Alien Fun Capsule
programme.desc: Madcap panel show presented by the comedian, with Jordan Stephens, Janet Street-Porter, Alex Brooker and Una Stubbs nominally tasked with saving planet Earth from any potential alien invasion. They must find clips, people and anything that is fun or funny enough for Harry to place in the capsule which, in the event that the planet is invaded by aliens, can be presented to them to demonstrate earthlings are good fun and therefore worthy of saving(n)

programme.title: Harry Hill's Alien Fun Capsule
programme.desc: The big-collared comedian returns with another round of madcap fun and laughter, joined by Emmerdale actor Gaynor Faye, Countryfile presenter Anita Rani, Professor Robert Winston and comedy legends Cannon and Ball. Their job is supposedly to help Harry fill a capsule with jokes, games, songs and clips to prove just how funny the human race can be and prevent an alien invasion - but in actual fact it's just an excuse to let Harry bring his particular brand of comedy back to prime-time television(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill raids the archives to present the 100 funniest home videos. The countdown includes clips of a faulty safety rope, a boy who is afraid of falling from a fairground ride, and a cat who can lip sync to music(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers featuring celebrity lookalikes, with kids using Brian May's scalp as a pinata, Orlando Bloom showing off his new truss and Mumford & Sons revealing how they keep in touch with the working classes(n)

programme.title: Hocus Pocus
programme.desc: A teenager in Salem, Massachusetts, breaks into a spooky house on Halloween and accidentally resurrects three witches who have been dead for 300 years. The sorceresses are soon causing chaos while seeking a suitable sacrifice for a ritual to give them eternal youth. However, an old foe - a boy they turned into a cat - is on hand to help stop their mischief. Supernatural Disney comedy, with Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker, Kathy Najimy and Omri Katz(n)

They must find clips, people and anything that is fun or funny enough for Harry to place in the capsule which, in the event that the planet is invaded by aliens, can be presented to them to demonstrate earthlings are good fun and therefore worthy of saving(n)

programme.title: Harry Hill's Alien Fun Capsule
programme.desc: The big-collared comedian returns with another round of madcap fun and laughter, joined by Emmerdale actor Gaynor Faye, Countryfile presenter Anita Rani, Professor Robert Winston and comedy legends Cannon and Ball. Their job is supposedly to help Harry fill a capsule with jokes, games, songs and clips to prove just how funny the human race can be and prevent an alien invasion - but in actual fact it's just an excuse to let Harry bring his particular brand of comedy back to prime-time television(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!

programme.title: Hocus Pocus

Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver(n)

programme.sub-title: The Theft of the Royal Ruby

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: Welsh comedian and presenter Griff Rhys Jones presents a compilation of never-seen-before out-takes from the world of film and TV. This edition's selection includes fluffs and foul-ups from hit shows such as period drama Mr Selfridge and medical comedy Doc Martin, as well as fantasy series Jonathan Strange and Mr Norrell, in which a horse steals the scene. Among the stars in the spotlight are actress Helen Mirren, weather presenter Carol Kirkwood, TV presenter Vernon Kay and actor Richard E Grant(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.desc: A chance to see day one of the inaugural event from Claudelands Arena in Hamilton, featuring all eight first-round matches, played over the best of 11 legs. programme.title: Secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: An archaeological team makes a discovery that exposes a state-sanctioned looting scandal, while in Cairo, experts in antiquities work with Interpol to crack down on the black market trade in stolen relics. A dig run by Spanish archaeologists makes an unusual discovery - a 3000-year-old mummified heart(n)

programme.title: Prince Albert: A Victorian Hero Revealed
programme.desc: Professor Saul David examines Prince Albert's significant influence on British culture, governmental policy and even international relations in his short life. The queen's consort faced an uphill struggle to be taken seriously by a nation and parliament that viewed him with hostility, but by the time of his death at the age of 42, he was widely regarded as a forward-thinking reformer. The programme draws on the private papers in the Royal Archives to gain a full picture of his legacy(n)

programme.title: Prince Philip: The Plot to Make a King
programme.desc: Secret History documentary telling the inside story of the dynastic tensions that were unleashed when Princess Elizabeth, the future queen, fell in love with Prince Philip. It was felt that Philip was rough and ill-mannered - and with the Second World War still fresh in most people's minds, the royal and political elite disliked his German roots. But following the death of King George VI in 1952, the establishment feared a dynastic coup, with the Queen torn between the two sides. The documentary examines the complexities and frustrations of the royal marriage, as the monarch struggled to reconcile the love of her husband with the suspicions of her family and her government(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Lost Family
programme.desc: Britain is ripped apart by the abdication of Edward VIII and debate rages in parliament about Republicanism, while Nazi Germany continues to be of concern. Edward makes a trip with Wallis Simpson to Germany and meets Hitler, but when war is declared, his hopes of a high-profile position in the war effort are dashed, and he is instead given governorship of the Bahamas. Now he truly is exiled(n)

programme.title: WW2: The True Cost of War

There is also a look at how the US rebuilt its navy and took itself from the 18th-ranked army in the world to be second only to the Soviet Union, a tale of ingenuity and enterprise that turned the vast resources of America into the numbers that could win a war(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Crooked House
programme.desc: Adaptation of the mystery novel, starring Max Irons and Terence Stamp. Charles, the son of the assistant commissioner at Scotland Yard returns to London from Cairo following the end of the Second World War, only to discover his fiancee's grandfather has been murdered. He uses his father's connections to become involved in the investigation, and finds the dead man's second wife a likely suspect. programme.desc: Documentary following people around the UK working in waste disposal. programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: Workers at Crapper and Sons salvage what they can from the hundreds of tons of plastic that is brought in every month while 10,000 tons of compost is sold to local farmers every year. S Norton is a multi-million pound complex of hydraulic and electrical systems, where hundreds of people shred 1.5 million tons of steel per year, which is then shipped off to Turkey for use in steelworks around the world. Today(n)

programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the work of Refood, which diverts rotting food from landfill and plugs it into the National Grid. If all the food wasted by UK homes was turned into energy, it could power more than 1.2 million homes. There is also a look at Sue and John Wellfare, who have made it their mission to recycle the unrecyclable. The pair sort items by hand, from biscuit wrappers and baby-food sachets to crisp packets, and have set up drop-off points for what would otherwise end up on the ground(n)

Spirits run high with a trip to the beach and a game of bingo, but Nancy's complaints over dog hairs on the sheets and smoking in the rooms bring down the light-hearted mood during dinner(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Paul Billington is annoyed that the bed he and wife Flora Loizou are given is not big enough, but is more impressed when taken for food at a quirky local restaurant(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests stay with Caroline Scott, the owner of the Dean B&B near Longniddry, East Lothian The picturesque house and breathtaking views across the Firth of Forth win everyone over, but underwear in the drawers, cobwebs on the curtains and a messy breakfast service seriously hamper the host's chances of success(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer and Kirstie Allsopp are in south-east London with two couples for whom timing is critical. In and around the Isle of Dogs and Greenwich, Lia and Andrew have saved a big deposit and have a sizeable budget of £600,000 to spend. But, after witnessing the crash of 2008, Andrew is nervous of spending his hard-earned money for fear the market will dip. Lucy and Dan live in a B&B and if they don't find somewhere soon their vintage campervan will become their home. Kirstie shows the couple two potential properties, but the third option creates a reaction she isn't expecting and suddenly the search is thrown up in the air(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Teachers Nat and Tim find they always work and play a distance from their home in the Sydney suburb of Picnic Point. Tim wants to cut travel time by moving, but Nat wants to stay put. Neale Whitaker must move the kitchen and make the floor plan work better for their young family. Andrew Winter's job is to find a new house within budget that has the extras to lure Nat away from Team Love It(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Four Sumatran orang-utans escape from their quarters, and tempting them back inside proves easier said than done, while the dominant female meerkat faces trouble from her daughter, who is launching physical attacks upon her mother. A young Asian elephant keeps the rest of the herd entertained as they adapt to the loss of two members to a virus. The keepers believe it needs a playmate its own age, but their hopes rest on the bull elephant mating with the females once more(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick faces one of his greatest practical and ethical challenges yet when he's introduced to Hermes, a tortoise with only one good leg. Can he provide Hermes with three prosthetic feet, using tiny titanium implants? Meanwhile, a rottweiler called Dave urgently needs an upgrade to his previous hip replacements(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

Jonathan and Michael, who are struggling to turn their magnificent French castle into a money-making venture, explore some of their chateau's caves in the hope of finding a buried fortune. programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman with a weak immune system owing to the radiotherapy she underwent for breast cancer is bitten by her cat, a minor injury that could cause her serious problems.
